Copy blueprint - Week 6 - Session 11
Or
Copy blueprint – Week 6 - Session 2
The perfect pitch is needed to close the reader on the idea of taking action. This takes time
energy and effort on the part of the copywriter. The pitch must be an irresistible offer.
An irresistible offer is mandatory
The price point is something to pay attention to as it must be just right – too low or too high is
going to get resistance. Price points must be tested in order to make the process work.
Just as in face to face sales your internal beliefs about the offer will show up in the writing of the
offer. (In face to face sales whatever objection you fear the most is the one you will get
because you talk around it) If writing copy your internal beliefs will spill over onto the writing.
I must be able to detach myself from my fears and be the best advocate for the product or service
without the baggage of fear, I can always come back to concerns later. The sales process on
paper requires the ability to be a closer.
4:00
Creating copy from the formulas is not enough. Learn how the formulas are written so that I can
do them on my own.
Just like in face to face sales … If I don’t believe in what I have to offer the buyer will hear it
through my words and efforts.
This process is the opposite of empathy and I must be my ideal self (Nietzchesan says a Cesar
with Christs soul) – This is my ideal “super self” while working on the pitch. NOT
ARROGANT – Optimized – I must give myself mental permission to walk in with the mind of
a closer. No concerns, no neurosis, no fear – ready to sell. (If I pause and write a pitch without
the mental state the missing elements will be very apparent)
In week 1 Jon talked about empathy as a framework to understand – to writing the pitch I must
be a closer – There are 2 people internally writing the copy
1. 	For the copy an empathetic understanding individual
2. 	For the pitch – a freaking confident closer
7:30
There are 5 elements to the pitch:
1. 	Offer pre frame (this is the success elements in copy pro)
2. 	The offer USP (follow the formula) – elevator pitch
3. 	Sale bullets (follow the formula) Master the formula and then break the rules – top
copywriters start with the bullets – These are the boldest, intriguing and bizarre claims that can
be made. This is done to grab attention of the reader by the throat. In order to do this I must be in
the closer midset
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 9 --

4. 	Offer social proof (the testimonials and bonuses trick)
5. 	The offer guarantee (the triple guarantee and the 5 point check list)
Calls to action are coming in the next week
9:45
Preframe
The preframe technique is setting up your offer from the position of having (A) analyzed success
case studies within my niche and (B) the years I spent researching this to create the product.
Here is the framework to come from:
I am not perfect, I’m not – I have made mistakes as you saw in my story. I made mistakes and I
knew I had to lean on successful people. I had to stand on the shoulders of giants as the phrase is
often used. In order to do that I analyzed 150 of the most successful ________ people in the
world. I knew their success could be replicated. You know what It took me years to replicate it
and figure out how to teach that process and replicate their success. Guess what? I have done this
all for you.
This process is more compelling than “I have done this all myself”. NASA needed to use a
group of people to get astronauts home. The feel that it took multiple people to figure this out is
better than a “Self made man”. The buyer has a feel that multiple people are there to help them
out.
Look at all of the case studies I have gone through, the books, seminars and classes I have been
though to get to this point. – This information has to be included into the copy of a product or
service. (Think Schlitz beer and the water purification process)
This “preframes” the offer as something more than a pitch – it’s the collective sum of knowledge
the ultra successful than only I can bring to the table. – for the buyer this gives them the feeling
has been group sourced and not just figured out from me. Multiple leaders and experts are better
than just me alone. This is the library of knowledge
Jon explains the copypro example that 27 of the top copywriters were included in the product
and now the rest of the people who add to the “knowledge base”
I have studied the most successful. I have role modeled their approach and knowledge. I
perfected what I learned working with my own clients and customers.
This takes me out of having to be the one with all of the answers and psychologically preframes
the prospect to adopt a success mindset. Another way to say this is the story “primes” the reader
to take action like I have. Since this is a story they can input themselves into the action. All great
offers have a story element. – Its tough to think of failure at this point. The reader is looking for
something that is replicatable.
14:40
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 9 --

I will combine “success traits” within copypro with “offer preframe” to create a Kick Ass
preframe:
This is one of the ways to write the preframe – there are multiple ways to write this:
Preframe formula
Are there patterns of the truly successful? I analyzed __________ of the top success stories.
They all share one thing in common __________
It took time: __________ to perfect this system (blueprint / course / program). What resulted was
a “success blueprint” you can follow. One that works consistently. For virtually anyone. Time
and time again.
Introducing __________ (offer name)
When looking at success traits – look for the one thing that I want someone to believe.
There is “one thing” that needs to be explained not a million things. A system takes time to
perfect and doesn’t just happen over night.
16:45
Jons example with the blanks filled in:
Are there patterns of the truly successful? I analyzed over 100 of the top weight loss success
stories. They all share one thing in common: They varied their carbohydrate intake throughout
the week.
It took time: 14 years working with everyone from hopeless weight loss cases to professional
athletes to prefect this system. What resulted was a “success blueprint” you can follow. One that
works consistently. For virtually anyone. Time and time again.
Introducing the 4 day diet.
Thoughts on the pre frame:
Good formulas are not developed overnight in the mind of the reader. There problem has
persisted over time. Something that was too quick to create may disappoint them.
17:40
Places to put this:
This can be all in one place, condensed as I saw it on the other slide, or spread apart as it is
within the $50 million dollar sales letter. (this letter is in CopyPro)
This can be altered to fit whatever product or service I use. Perhaps other pros, for example. The
key to this preframe is the power of the role modeling success and the perfecting a “personal
blueprint” for them to follow. (I looked at all of the _________ (people – product) and I made it
easy for you – I took _________ year plus time, energy and effort you don’t have to do).
19:00
Unique selling proposition (USP)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 9 --

A USP is the elevator pitch of the offer.
This should be roughly 25 words and not 100
CopyPro has a USP formula
1. 	The turn (or transition)
2. 	The medium (how the product is offered)
3. 	The target (the avatar or expected avatar)
4. 	The big hairy promise (the biggest promise I have)
5. 	The without (Duality or my prospects objections – no more than three)
USP Example:
TURN: This resulted in the worlds first
MEDIUM: cyclical blueprint
TARGET: for men and women fat burners everywhere
PROMISE: That empowers you to lose up to 21 pounds in 21 days
WITHOUT: Without exercise, counting calories, or doing without your favorite foods.
(Jon mentions that claims like 21 pounds in 21 days can be buried in the copy and still
deliverable to a reader)
Any product needs a USP – If cant teach it I don’t know it. I must be able to state what I do
simply. Most people do not have a USP that makes sense or is easy to understand.
There needs to be something unique in the USP like “cyclical blueprint”
If I can’t be the first I can be:
· 	The world finest (subjective)
· 	The worlds premier (subjective)
If I can believe it I can say it (objectivity – subjectivity – do not over promise)
The trick for the USP insert:
Are there patterns of the truly successful? I analyzed __________ of the top success stories.
They all share one thing in common __________
It took time: __________ to perfect this system (blueprint / course / program). What resulted was
a “success blueprint” you can follow. One that works consistently. For virtually anyone. Time
and time again.
USP – Insert the USP here
Introducing __________ (offer name)
23:40
Buyers mindset
This process is used to help presell the buyer on the ideas about to be sold.
There is a buyers language like there is a love language.
24:00 - Bullets
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 9 --

Bullets are hard hitting promises, laced with a combination of hyper specific guarantees and
“teaser” that build insane curiosity.
Sales bullet formula is built into CopyPro. Jon used all of the greatest writers of bullets in
CopyPro (Carlton, Makepeace, Ogilvy and more)
Bullet Formula:
The lead: The super attention grabber (there are multiple ways to build a lead)
The verb: These must be juicy verbs when writing a bullet, stay away from adjectives as much
as possible. (Obliterate is better than destroy)
The Hook: the sexy promise (what it is
The turn: A pivot in the story, can be as simple as a “without”
The close: This is closed out with a promise or a CTA
Learn how to use the formula and then master the chaos of modifying the rules.
27:00
Examples of sales bullets:
· 	5 ways the “copy gurus” influence you negatively and lead you to copy failure mode!
· 	WARNING: Don’t even think of trying to increase sales and profits until you learn how
trying to write emails from scratch can sabotage your results!
· 	How to AUTOMATICALLY GENERATE high converting emails and sequences within
seconds.. and all without having to hire expensive copywriters!
· 	Are you making any of these 3 internet marketing mistakes? You need to click on “setup
/ QuickStart” – You might be surprised!
· 	You’ll find out how to avoid “blank page paralysis” – never again will you feel freaked
out by that dreaded blinking cursor of death or beaten down about copy creation!
· 	You’ll get a surefire trick for choosing the PERFECT POWER VERB – you’re gonna
love this!
· 	PLUS watch your copy convert like crazy using this 57-year-old “Mad Men” tactic –
you’ll even amaze yourself with this neat little trick!
These bullets were all built to create curiosity in the reader
30:00
Not all bullets follow the formula, they all work around it some way
In CopyPro start with the ONLY formula (the bugs will be updated in CopyPro 2.0)
Use the drop down boxes until I find something that I like.
32:00 – Social proof
· 	Social proof can make or break an offer. The stronger the proof, the easier it is to sell
· 	Proof can be scarce, or even sometime nonexistent
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 9 --

· 	Tips for getting testimonials fast:
o Offer a bonus in exchange for an honest testimonial. (Jon did this for his podcast and received
200 reviews overnight) Email customers (or friends who have tried my product if I have no
customers) offer a nice bonus for a honest testimonial or social review. Facebook reviews are
great as they carry instant trust. (take screenshots as a way to show social proof – pull quotes) 3
or 4 really good ones work well
o Write a series of testimonials for customers to click on (with a bonus offering if need be) and
frame it as a survey. Make sure you have 10-20 prewritten testimonials and ask the customer to
choose the one that “best represents their experience” (people love to take surveys – Ask method
teaches to use the work quiz instead or survey)
o Most people want to tell me good stuff. It is easier to think of bad stuff. Pre frame what they
could write a testimonial about. (It gave me X without Y or Z)
o What is your Facebook page? Can I use your picture inside your testimonial
o Do the survey on Facebook – public domain to be used all I want.
· 	Get testimonials for the bonuses used. This is a triple whammy. First it gives my prospect
multiple reasons to buy. If they are not already sold on the primary offer, a bonus with a killer
testimonial (I would have paid 2X Just for this bonus) can push someone over the buying edge.
Second, it adds monster social proof to my offer. Third, I can sell all of my bonuses after the
offer closes and use the testimonials as social proof.
o These testimonials can be pre written as examples for the person to write.
o As the buyers scrolls they see picture / proof. They don’t know what the items are for if the
reader is scrolling. This can be done for every aspect of business. The customer support,
interaction with a employee.
o Set up pages for the bonuses to be sold. Add the testimonials into the offer page of for the
bonuses offered.
39:45 – Offer guarantee
· 	If I am going to use a guarantee I have to make it count
· 	Jon has removed guarantees from offers and have not suffered in sales, this only works
for high dollar offers. (this can be phrases in the right way)
· 	If I must offer a guarantee (low dollar offer, super competitive space, etc) this I can do
one of two things:
1. 	Make it as bold and darling as possible. Jason Fladliens offer of you can call my bank to
verify there is $1,000,000 to cover any and all returns. He uses a picture of a certified letter form
the bank. A pull quote is used “Rapid Crush has money stored in escrow for any product buy
backs – if you don’t believe me call the bank yourself
People do actually call the banker. “If this doesnt work for you, we will buy back all of the
product we sold you”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 9 --

2. 	Use the triple guarantee approach (this can be teased early in the letter to get more
attention as well) This can be a play on words.
· 	The Triple guarantee is 3 ways to get their money back (this is not a triple your money
back guarantee)
Way #1: Big Hairy promise plus a timeline: “If you don’t X with Y days, I insist on giving
your money back. You’d be an outlier, but on the off chance the super-unexpected happens, I
couldn’t sleep at night if I took a penny of your money”
Way #2: The easy peasy promise: “OR if this isnt the easiest _________ plan EVER..”
(this may be hard work but it is super easy to follow)
Way #3: Even if you don’t like the font! “OR EVEN… you don’t like anything about the
_________ (offer) even the FONT I used! For any reason AT ALL, your purchase is 100%
secure today” (there isnt any talk of risk since this thought isnt wanted.)
47:35 - 5 point checklist guarantee
Give the prospect a downloadable PDF “guarantee checklist”. If they don’t see (promise 1, 2, 3,
4, 5) and if all check boxes not checked off within ________ days, they get their money back
This is all future pacing of the guarantee: “Picture this: its 7 days from now. Your waist is at
least 2 inches smaller! Check that box the moment you notice it” (this is explained as if the task
has been done – not if)
End with “If you don’t not check ALL FIVE, I insist on giving you a full refund the very day
you let me know. (personal wording is key most people are expecting “email customer support”.)
(not in the course) The inverse - Sir Gary Halbert used this to get people to focus on using the
product. In similar fashion Joe Polish has the person buying Piranha Marketing call in to register
the physical product and give instructions. This action reduced the return rate.
John Carlton would get people to return the work book they used to prove they worked through
the course in Simple Writing System as a condition of the guarantee.
(back to the class)
51:00
The price point must be right
If the higher price point wins … cool
If the lower test point wins refund the difference.
52:00 – The home work
Log into Copy Pro – look for sales pages bonus – select both and start a project
Use $50 million sales letter blue print to find and fill in success traits, offer preframe and offer
USP. Study how they are used in the sales letter structure.
If homework has been done to date mostly everything has been filled in.
Use direct response sales letter and fill in the sale bullets.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 9 --

Write my own triple guarantee or 5 point check list (neither template has it – the triple guarantee
is in Sellerator)
This is the first writing assignment in the course – Jon like the 5 point checklist because of the
use of future pacing.
55:00 Questions and answers
LeAnn comments – Do people have to check with merchant accounts for guarantees – Click
back requires 60 day money back guarantee. Jon says the longer the guarantee the less refunds.
Jon also uses a reverse 5 point checklist where the person has to take action – The only way I
will give you the money back is if _________ number of conditions has been met.
Low dollar products 5 point – high dollar reverse 5 point
Include commands for the buyer so they know what to do – “Put this on your mirror” or “put this
on your refrigerator so you can know every day what you can expect from this product” the odds
of implementation go up with a process like this
The font guarantee is a favorite
58:15
Elaine
Testimonials – Do beta testers work?
Use only when true and this does work. CopyPro used this process 3 years ago.
Scarcity is next week and is easy to line out.
There are tactics that can be used to make scarcity work
60:00
Feedback on the course
LeAnn – testimonials can be used to handle the objections buyers have – match up guarantees
with the objection the buyer may have.
Objections can also be dealt with inside the 5 point checklist
The preframe helps the buyer make a buying decision easier
Dovi – Did Sherpas client ever use the 5 point guarantee – they did. Get them to print it out,
(Make it a downloadable pdf, audio recording or even an accountability video.)
102:00
Erection pills used the line … “If your erection lasts over 4 hours go to the Dr” this was done as
a warning as well as a way to show the benefit of the pill. This is a real concern but it has the
benefit of saying the drug is powerful.

“You will discover __________ (preframe) to __________ USP”
106:30
Dovi – Can you use a refund testimonial? Jon has never used this but says it is Samurai to take
this type of action.
The more testimonials in possession the easier it is to sell
Keep a file of testimonials
Testimonials can also be used to identify avatars or buyers – they can also be targeted to the
avatar needed. Having specific testimonials to avatar does help with conversions.
Elaine Give yourself permission to succeed. Where am I not giving myself permission to
succeed? This is a subconscious issue as well as a conscious for most people. “I could never be
__________ (type of person) what are the barriers that hold people back. This needs to be in the
offer and the close for both the copywriter as well as the avatar.
The Jesus – Caesar conversation helped understand how to get past empathy.
Be the person I need to be to close the sale and then I can go back to be someone else after I have
completed the copy.
Permission is a mental thing when tied to an outcome
I just broke up with my girlfriend so I will eat some cake
I just worked out really hard for a month so I will eat some cake
I must write compelling copy so I can go back to myself when I am done
1;13:00
Mic Jager lyrics paint a picture in little words (he could be a great writer)
The lyrics have rhythm – so does good copy – this is why VSLs work so well.

People like the course
Will you give yourself permission to __________ (action)
There must be belief that the product is great and can help people –

What to do if I don’t think it is the best product – “There are other products out there that do the
job.. But I am telling you right now I am not going to be satisfied with this product as it is, so I
am going to give you lifetime updates”.
Learn how to voice every objection into a positive
